findings.summary: Setting shouldClaimAndClose: false in buildRemoveLiquidityTransactions skips both ClaimFee and PDA close.
findings.solution: Explicitly call buildClaimFeesTransactions() before remove liquidity.
findings.lessons: Meteora state transitions for ClaimFee and RemoveLiquidity are separate. Explicit call sequence is required.

findings.summary: Stranded fees occur if harvest is skipped before Whirlpool close.
findings.solution: Enforce explicit harvest step before principal removal.
findings.lessons: Harvest/Claim should always be an explicit step in close sequence to ensure error feedback.

findings.summary: PDA closure without harvest results in unrecoverable reward tokens.
findings.lessons: Sequence-based close is the industry standard for CLMM position cleanup.

recommendation.reason: Bug confirmed. The current implementation in solana-executor.ts:113 skips fee claim. High risk of capital leakage.
recommendation.alternative_approach: Option B (Explicit Sequence) is already implemented in PR #25. It adds buildClaimFeesTransactions call before remove liquidity.

summary: SolanaExecutor.close() skips fee claim because shouldClaimAndClose is set to false in buildRemoveLiquidityTransactions. Sub-tasks confirm this is a known CLMM pattern requiring explicit ClaimFee sequence.
root_cause_hypothesis: The shouldClaimAndClose flag skips both ClaimFee and PDA close. A separate closePosition() call only closes empty PDA, leaving fees stranded.
gaps_found: buildClaimFeesTransactions is missing from the close sequence
Zero-fee positions should be handled gracefully (no-op)
new_bugs_risk: Adding claimFees is safe. Zero-fee catch handles cases where no fees are accumulated.
